output: Implemented the Sprint 4 rework: tightened sprint lock-in validation, added deterministic confirmation parsing, added focused tests, and ran targeted plus full-suite validation. Full suite still has one unrelated dirty-worktree failure in the no-secret scanner.

I did not repair unrelated `.megaplan` state.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the migration coverage through the focused adapter/migration tests under Python 3.11.11; the existing SQLite and Supabase sprint migration assertions passed.
Verified sprint CRUD, item replacement/listing, helper loading, and hot-context sprint flags through the focused store tests under Python 3.11.11.

Fixed explicit lock-in validation so unknown sprint numbers, duplicate queued assignments, and queued/pending conflicts fail before mutation. Added deterministic lock-in confirmation parsing and tests. The temporary reproduction script confirmed an unknown explicit lock-in sprint now raises `SprintValidationError`.
Focused Sprint 4 tests verify shaping/planned gate blockers and lockdown phrase behavior, including Open Questions and fenced-code exemptions.

Focused Sprint 4 tests verify `edit_epic` state advancement, forced handoff logging, sprint writes, lock-in, and `planned_at` assignment. The new lock-in validation is exercised through `edit_epic` and rolls back cleanly on invalid payloads.
Revert coverage in the Sprint 4 tests confirms sprint queue/reorder history is restored from stored prior sprint snapshots without disturbing the existing revert path.
Focused tests confirm `get_epic` returns current sprints and `get_epic_at_time` exposes historical sprint state from replay snapshots.
Envelope tests in the focused run confirm completed invocation envelopes report actual post-turn state plus `state_transition` and `sprint_changes` after Sprint 4 edits.
System prompt and end-of-turn focused tests passed, covering the sprint flow guidance and phase-aware checks already present in the prompt/context path.

Added focused tests for explicit lock-in validation and confirmation parsing. The Sprint 4 test module now covers the reworked edge case plus existing lifecycle, force-through, queue/reorder, lockdown, and envelope behavior.

Ran targeted Sprint 4/adapter/envelope/end-of-turn/prompt tests successfully under Python 3.11.11 and ran the full suite. Full suite has one unrelated pre-existing dirty-worktree failure in the no-secret scanner reading a deleted `.megaplan` file; no Sprint 4 tests failed. Temporary reproduction script was run and deleted.
commands_run: ~/.pyenv/versions/3.11.11/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_sprints.py tests/test_sqlite_store.py tests/test_supabase_adapters.py tests/test_envelope.py tests/test_end_of_turn.py tests/test_system_prompt.py -q

Focused tests passed for `edit_epic` sprint/state writes, `planned_at`, forced handoff logging, blockers, and rollback on invalid sprint input.
Focused tests passed for reverting sprint queue/reorder changes from prior sprint snapshots.
Focused tests passed for current `get_epic` sprint visibility and historical `get_epic_at_time` sprint reconstruction.

Prompt/end-of-turn focused tests passed; prompt text includes the two-beat lock-in flow, blocker options, queue/pend/reorder guidance, and all-pending warning behavior.
Added direct tests for explicit lock-in validation and confirmation parsing; existing Sprint 4 tests cover the listed acceptance criteria without production Supabase credentials.
Targeted validation passed and the temporary reproduction script was deleted. Full suite was run; one unrelated dirty-worktree no-secret scanner failure remains.
The hosted Supabase migration action is clearly identified as manual-only and was not performed in this session.
